pi_items.source: Task description in task.json states: 'Save the construction as 'polar_curves.ggb' in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/'; export_result.sh script verifies file at /home/ga/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/polar_curves.ggb

Mathematical verification: Three-petal rose r = sin(3θ) is well-established (Wikipedia Rose (mathematics), Mathworld Rose Curve); task uses Curve(sin(3t) * cos(t), sin(3t) * sin(t), t, 0, 2π); pattern 'sin(3' appears in the formula; verifier searches for 'sin(3' in normalized XML

Mathematical verification: Cardioid r = 1 + cos(θ) is well-established (Wikipedia Cardioid, BYJUS mathematics); task uses Curve((1 + cos(t)) * cos(t), (1 + cos(t)) * sin(t), t, 0, 2π); pattern '1+cos' appears in the formula; verifier searches for '1+cos' in normalized XML
Task description explicitly states: 'add at least two text labels identifying the curves'; verifier logic checks for text_count >= 2 to award full 20 points

privileged_info_summary: This task requires creating a polar curve gallery in GeoGebra with four elements: (1) a three-petal rose generated by r = sin(3θ) using parametric Curve(sin(3t)*cos(t), sin(3t)*sin(t), t, 0, 2π), (2) a cardioid generated by r = 1 + cos(θ) using parametric Curve((1+cos(t))*cos(t), (1+cos(t))*sin(t), t, 0, 2π), (3) a parameterized rose with slider n ∈ [1,8] using Curve(sin(n*t)*cos(t), sin(n*t)*sin(t), t, 0, 2π), and (4) at least two text annotations. All curves are created synthetically within GeoGebra using the correct polar-to-Cartesian conversions (x = r*cos(θ), y = r*sin(θ)). The file must be saved as 'polar_curves.ggb' in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/. Verification checks for the presence of pattern 'sin(3' for the three-petal rose and '1+cos' for the cardioid in the resulting .ggb XML file.
